hero.lede: Backend engineer with 3+ years building high-throughput platforms, latency-critical APIs, and MCP/RAG-powered agents that triage real incidents and keep production inside SLA.

metrics.label: api latency cut
metrics.sub: p99 on hot path · MySQL JSON index

bioBlock: > 3+ yrs · SDE @ Locus (IKEA), prev. Clover Bay
> stack: Java · Spring Boot · Go · Python · Kafka · MySQL
> ai infra: MCP · Claude 3.5 · RAG · Ollama · vector search
- cut prod CPU 15% via MySQL multi-value JSON index
- shipped MCP context proxy (Go) for agent grounding
- autonomous remediation for 200+ unique alert types
> currently: WhatsApp CRM backend · MCP proxy v2

services.name: svc-incident-agent
services.summary: MCP + Claude 3.5 workflow that triages production alerts autonomously by injecting live codebase context, historical Slack mitigations, telemetry traces, and diagnostic playbooks into each investigation.

star.situation: 200+ unique alert types across NewRelic / Opsgenie / Groundcover. Each one drained 30–60 min of engineer time digging through logs, code, and prior Slack threads.
star.task: cut human investigation toil while still meeting a hard 15-minute SLA on critical alerts — without changing the alerting source.
star.action: built an MCP-tool stack the agent can call: live code search (semantic + grep), Slack mitigation history lookup, trace/log correlation, Jira auto-creation. Slack daemon orchestrates dispatch; Devin handles long-running fixes.
star.result: ~70% drop in manual investigation time · 200+ alert types now triaged autonomously · 15-min critical-SLA held without paging an engineer.

services.name: svc-mcp-context-proxy
services.summary: Go localhost HTTP proxy intercepting Claude Code's MCP calls, caching upstream responses, and injecting token-budgeted code context from a local vector index into every tool response.

star.situation: agents calling MCP tools had no grounding in private codebases. Every prompt re-paid the cost of fetching context, and re-runs of identical calls hit the upstream provider needlessly.
star.task: deliver repo-aware, token-budgeted context to every MCP tool call without modifying the IDE, the agent, or the upstream MCP servers.
star.action: Go proxy on localhost:7070 · SQLite response cache keyed by source + request-body hash · file watcher for incremental re-index · Ollama embeddings + cosine top-k search · /patterns/feedback loop promotes query shapes that produced useful Groundcover results.
star.result: 71% cache hit rate on repeat tool calls · ~8ms median context-injection latency · pattern catalog converts oncall sessions into reusable institutional memory.

services.name: svc-journey-planner
services.summary: Distributed permanent journey planning service that lets N concurrent field managers build weekly route plans against shared inventory of stops, without serial locking and without double-allocation.

star.situation: multiple field managers concurrently authored weekly journey plans against the same pool of stops. Naïve locking forced them into a serial queue; no locking caused double-allocation and downstream conflicts.
star.task: support concurrent route authoring at scale while honouring territory, capacity, and visit-frequency constraints — and keep a defensible audit trail for every assignment.
star.action: optimistic-locking allocation with bounded exponential backoff on conflict · per-stop ownership state with versioned writes · constraint engine for territory / capacity / visit-freq rules · append-only audit log for every assignment and conflict-resolution decision.
star.result: 95% of routes generated without human intervention · 15+ hrs saved per manager per week · zero double-allocation observed in audit window.

services.name: svc-json-mv-index
services.summary: MySQL 8.0.17+ multi-value index over a nested JSON array column, migrating a high-traffic hot query from full JSON scan to direct B-tree lookup.

star.situation: a high-QPS hot query filtered rows by membership in a JSON-array column. Every call triggered a full column scan + JSON parse across millions of rows. p99 latency climbed, CPU saturated nightly, false-positive pager alerts followed.
star.task: bring the hot path under an index without denormalising the JSON column, splitting the table, or rewriting upstream callers.
star.action: added a MySQL 8.0.17+ multi-value index over the array path · rewrote the predicate from JSON_CONTAINS to MEMBER OF · verified EXPLAIN showed index use · rolled out behind a feature flag with shadow-traffic comparison.
star.result: ~15% reduction in prod CPU on the affected stack · p99 on the hot query dropped from ~740ms → ~11ms · 20–30 false-positive pager alerts/week eliminated.

services.name: svc-whatsapp-crm
services.summary: Multi-tenant CRM built on the WhatsApp Business API — horizontal scaling with isolated tenant data, conversation state management, and webhook-driven message routing.

star.situation: SMBs needed a CRM on WhatsApp Business API but couldn't justify per-tenant infrastructure cost; existing offerings co-mingled tenant data or required heavyweight onboarding.
star.task: ship a multi-tenant CRM with strict tenant isolation, idempotent webhook handling, and a clean horizontal-scale boundary — runnable on a single GCP project.
star.action: Spring Boot service on GCP · schema-per-tenant isolation in PostgreSQL · idempotent webhook consumer keyed by message-id · finite-state machine modelling conversation lifecycle · async outbound delivery worker decoupled from inbound webhook ack path.
star.result: currently in canary · sub-second webhook ack on inbound messages · clean per-tenant scaling boundary, ready for first design-partner rollout.

deploys.body: Agentic observability workflows, autonomous remediation daemons, distributed journey planning, high-load queueing optimizations, MySQL multi-value JSON index migrations cutting prod CPU by 15%.
Reactive Spring Boot notification infrastructure, Kafka-based async delivery, Couchbase/Redis e-commerce systems, Python bulk APIs on GCP, services with 90% test coverage.
